note: Preserved output of the BLOCKED first Cycle-1 audit (attempt 1). Kept under a non-canonical filename so wfi-audit-cycle resumption logic (which keys on WFI-003-auditor-a.json) starts Cycle 1 fresh after revision.

checks.note: All citations verified against the retrospective, RUN-20260705T023011Z-sdd-forge-mcp.json (gate_reports.total=0, review_tickets.major=1 confirmed verbatim), spot-checked T-001 implementation/gate reports, and RT-20260623-001.yml whose target.feature is claude-workflow-compatibility, corroborating the misattribution claim.

Missing-specification mechanism: authors follow gate report conventions while the measurement pipeline expects an additive identity schema documented nowhere in project workflow files; drift uniform across all authors and tasks.

'Category: measurement' is not a recognized category: the governing guide plugins/sdd-quality-loop/references/wfi-category-guide.md (171 lines) defines only plugin-improvement and app-dev-efficiency; there is no Section 5 and no 'strict audit lane'. Additionally (conditional on plugin-improvement language rules): 'quality-gate reports' in the Change Description is a Section 2 forbidden term; required substitution 'quality verification gate reports'.

Target AGENTS.md exists; field-level description with ledger equality constraint. Minor: AGENTS.md has no subsection literally named 'reporting conventions'; the actual target section should be named.
Quantitative: 33 N/A cells -> 0; gate_reports.total from 0 to the on-disk report count.
One primary metric, Baseline 33, Target 0, Horizon next feature run (issue #64). Minor: 33 cells vs FP-002's 22 artifacts are different granularities and deserve a one-sentence reconciliation.
Re-apply deterministic artifact rules at the next retrospective; cross-check run-record gate_reports.total against on-disk report count; judgment-free counts.

Adds ledger-verifiable identity metadata only; no grading logic, PASS/FAIL criteria, or thresholds change. Run ID values must equal ids reserved in the append-only identity ledger, so the fields cannot be fabricated to inflate counts; the change re-exposes already-verified work to the counting pipeline. Residual Minor risk: the metric tracks N/A-cell count, not the accuracy of newly populated values; bounded by the ledger cross-check.

findings.description: 'Category: measurement' and the cited 'strict audit lane, wfi-category-guide.md §5' do not exist in the governing guide, which defines only plugin-improvement and app-dev-efficiency. The WFI is unclassifiable under the existing guide.
Forbidden term 'quality-gate' (describing the gate concept) in the Change Description; required substitution 'quality verification gate'.
'Add ... to the reporting conventions' presumes an AGENTS.md subsection that does not exist; name the actual target section.
Baseline 33 (cells) vs retrospective FP-002's 22 (artifacts) are different countings of the same root cause and need a one-sentence reconciliation.

proposed_revisions.revision: Reclassify as 'Category: plugin-improvement' (friction concerns the plugin-side measurement pipeline handoff) and bring prose sections into Section 2 generic-language compliance; or first extend the guide with a genuine measurement category, then re-audit.
Replace 'quality-gate reports must carry' with 'quality verification gate reports must carry'; replace 'to the reporting conventions' with a named existing AGENTS.md section.
Add a sentence reconciling the 33-cell baseline with FP-002's 22-artifact count.
